behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff: the classifier may build/forward an incomplete label list even if local helper logic is correct. A wiring test with the classifier + request builder + mocked API transport catches this. Real OS/network is not required, and correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate classifier with taxonomy, invoke classify(), capture outbound API payload via in-process test double, assert payload contains the full configured taxonomy labels every time.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit would miss regressions where labels are dropped between builder and API client call.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is local normalization/validation logic on returned label value. It can be triggered with deterministic inputs and does not require real infra or model variability.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/response-mapping function (or smallest public boundary) with taxonomy and invalid label (e.g., "sports"), assert output is "unknown".
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels were rejected because behavior text targets output mapping, not transport/retry/network/model-quality effects.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Pure determinism/idempotence of normalization for identical input; single-function/object boundary with no I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization repeatedly (or property test) on the same raw API payload and taxonomy; assert identical final label across runs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration/System/Agentic disqualified by behavior text: it explicitly fixes identical raw responses and tests normalization step only.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI API outputs. A stub would hide nondeterministic provider/model behavior and cannot validate consistency under real model responses.
behaviors.test_description: Execute N repeated real provider calls for the same document+taxonomy, record raw provider response and final label, assert final label stability when raw provider response is unchanged; allow changes only when provider response differs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System was considered but rejected because the key risk is model-output variability (LLM-dependent), which is above generic HTTP/OS behavior.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between token-budget logic and dispatch path (e.g., over-budget request still sent, or wrong truncate/reject action). In-process wiring test is sufficient; no real network/OS/model judgment required.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low token budget, call classify() with oversized document, assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) and verify dispatch behavior via mocked transport (sent truncated payload or not sent).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit was considered but rejected: behavior text includes "before dispatch," requiring verification of handoff/gating across modules.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real provider accounting from an AI API call; mocks can trivially report compliant usage and miss real tokenizer/model-side drift. The execution path includes a real model API call, so Integration is too low under LLM-DEP.
behaviors.test_description: Run classification against real provider with representative document, read provider-reported usage metadata, assert total tokens <= configured budget threshold (with fixed prompt/taxonomy fixture).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration rejected because behavior depends on real provider-reported usage, not just local routing/retry logic.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is retry/backoff orchestration across client wrapper, timing/backoff policy, and error mapping. This is catchable with in-process stubbed API responses and controllable clock/sleeper. Real OS/network not strictly required for minimum failure detection.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub provider: first response is rate-limit error, second is success; verify classifier invokes wait/backoff mechanism and returns successful label instead of propagating immediate error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System considered but rejected because behavior text does not require real network timing anomalies; retry policy correctness is testable in-process.
